TextENGNew: What remained was the cleanup.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Having come this far, I was resolved. Work like this didn't faze me.
changeReason: Old contains displaced/misaligned text unrelated to the Japanese source; new accurately renders the original.

TextENGNew: After all, getting rid of them had freed me from every last one of my problems.
changeReason: Old contains displaced/misaligned text unrelated to the Japanese source; new accurately renders the original.

TextENGNew: If this was the price of that freedom... I could endure it.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...Don't look back.@k If I stopped now, I'd never be able to take another step forward.
changeReason: Old contains displaced/misaligned text unrelated to the Japanese source; new accurately renders the original.

TextENGNew: A tar-black something clung to my entire body, so heavy it threatened to paralyze both movement and thought.
changeReason: Old contains displaced/misaligned text unrelated to the Japanese source; new accurately renders the original.

TextENGNew: What I could do now@k ...was simply to keep believing.
changeReason: Old contains displaced/misaligned text unrelated to the Japanese source; new accurately renders the original.

TextENGNew: To believe that what I'd done—no, what I'd accomplished—here in this junk heap was the only choice I had.
changeReason: Old contains displaced/misaligned text unrelated to the Japanese source; new accurately renders the original.

TextENGNew: ...Come to think of it, there was that murder case right here at the dam site, at the end of the dam conflict years ago.
changeReason: Old contains displaced/misaligned text unrelated to the Japanese source; new accurately renders the original.

TextENGNew: At first, I thought about dumping the remains in the stream that ran through the dam site.
But if I did that, someone downstream would definitely find them.
I'd seen news stories about remains washing up on a riverbank somewhere.
changeReason: Old contains displaced/misaligned text unrelated to the Japanese source; new accurately renders the original.

TextENGNew: If I went deep past the abandoned districts around Yagouchi or Takatsudo, there were any number of unexplored mountains.
Somewhere in those mountains should be safe enough.
...Don't stop. Keep moving forward.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...Don't look back. Eyes ahead.
changeReason: Old contains displaced/misaligned text unrelated to the Japanese source; new accurately renders the original.

TextENGNew: ...Using muscles I wasn't accustomed to had left my whole body aching.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: I cleaned up carefully, then retreated into my hideout and lay down on the mattress.
...The clammy sweat on my forehead plastered my bangs to my skin. It felt terrible.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: I'd take a bath later.@k A long, slow one... washing everything away.
When I reflected on what I'd been doing... I felt like I might be the worst criminal of the century.
changeReason: Old contains displaced/misaligned text unrelated to the Japanese source; new accurately renders the original.

TextENGNew: But... was there any other way?
I wouldn't use the word "never," but... I hadn't done this because I wanted to commit a crime.
I hadn't done anything wrong.
I'd done it to protect Dad's life and mine.
Anyone who'd condemn me was just a spectator on the far shore.
changeReason: New translation renders 対岸の人間 as 'spectator on the far shore,' preserving the Japanese metaphor of someone uninvolved watching from across the river.

TextENGNew: In the end, maybe no one can truly judge sin.@k Sin isn't something to be judged—it's something to acknowledge and atone for.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Which means only the person who committed the sin can measure its weight.
Did I feel remorse? Regret?
changeReason: New translation distinguishes 悔悟 (remorse) and 後悔 (regret) as separate concepts rather than paraphrasing both as 'regret/sorry.'

TextENGNew: ...No. Of course not.
Even now, I didn't believe I'd done anything wrong.
I could state with certainty: there had been no better course of action.
And besides... from the very night I killed those two, my life had already begun to change.
When I came home after killing Teppei.
...Dad was tidying the wrecked living room, his face swollen from crying.
``...Reina... where did you go...?``

``I spoke with that man.@k@vS09/02/170200369. I told him Rina-san will never come to this house again.@k@vS09/02/170200370. So there's no reason for him to come either.@k@vS09/02/170200371. He won't be back.``
``I doubt that's enough to keep someone like him away...@k@vS09/00/172900028. You should be careful for a while too, Reina...``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Explaining to Dad why Teppei would never return was difficult.
...I obviously couldn't say "Don't worry, I killed him."
I took ice from the freezer, packed it into a plastic bag, wrapped it in a towel, and held it out to Dad.
``Here, Dad.@k@vS09/02/170200371_2. ...Your cheek is really swollen.``
changeReason: New translation corrects 'cheeks' (plural) to 'cheek' (singular), matching the Japanese.

TextENGNew: He touched his own cheek and seemed to realize for the first time how swollen it was.
``...Oh... thank you.``
``I'll clean up, so don't worry about it.@k@vS09/02/170200373. Let me get the vacuum.``
He called after me, as if to stop me.
``...I'm sorry.@k@vS09/00/172900034. ...I'm truly, truly sorry.``
``It's okay.@k@vS09/02/170200375. ...Their whole business was deceiving people.@k@vS09/02/170200376. ...You were just one of their targets, Dad.``
``...Reina... you knew what they were?``
``...Yes. Only recently, though.@k@vS09/02/170200378. I didn't say anything because I thought you wouldn't believe me.@k@vS09/02/170200379. I'm sorry.``
He looked down.
When he'd brought up the remarriage, I'd told him those two were criminals running a badger game.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: But he'd refused to listen.
So saying he wouldn't have believed me had been accurate.
``She took advantage of you while you were still hurting from the divorce, Dad.``
changeReason: New translation adds the explicit context of the divorce (離婚), which is present in the Japanese but omitted in the original translation.

TextENGNew: ``...I'm... a terrible father.``
Then, as if to atone, Dad began telling me the story of how he and Rina had met.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: It was during his darkest days, when he couldn't face the bitter memories without the crutch of alcohol.
changeReason: New translation renders 酒の力を借りなければ (without borrowing the power of alcohol) more literally, emphasizing dependence rather than casual drinking.

TextENGNew: He'd wandered into a bar, and Rina happened to be the hostess who served him.
Listening to customers was her job.
She must have lent a sympathetic ear to his divorce story and offered comforting words.
An emotionally stable person would have taken it as just in-the-moment courtesy,@k ...but Dad, desperate for salvation, must have felt it as the beginning of something new.
After that, it was a pitifully predictable story.
Wanting to believe it was destiny, Dad viewed everything about Rina through rose-colored glasses.
...It would be easy to laugh and say he was dreaming.@k But that weakness is simply proof of being human. There was nothing to be done about it.
He'd had opportunities to question her dubious spending habits.
...But Dad had even interpreted that as her trying to cheer him up.
He bought her whatever she wanted, willingly becoming her puppet.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Bringing an unattached woman into the home he shared with his daughter was beyond inappropriate.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...But by then, Dad was so infatuated that such concerns didn't even cross his mind.
``...I'd... forgotten that I was a father first.@k@vS09/00/172900038. ...What was I thinking...``
He said, and began to sob.
I gently put my arm around his frail, trembling shoulders.
``It's all right.@k@vS09/02/170200382. ...If you've realized that, then you're still my father.@k@vS09/02/170200383. ...You're the only family I have left.``
Dad could only manage choked sobs.
...And so we held each other quietly, shedding tears together...
The next morning, Dad was up remarkably early.
By the time I woke, he was already in the kitchen, attempting to make breakfast.
To my astonishment, he said:
``Your father is getting his act together, starting today.``
``Ah... ahahahahahaha.@k@vS09/02/170200385. But, Dad, you might want to put some oil in the pan before you fry the eggs.``
``Oh!@k@vS09/00/172900041. ...Ahahahaha, it's been so long since I've been in the kitchen... ahahaha.``
Dad scraped the half-burnt eggs off the pan with a spatula and belatedly added oil.
It was too late for that.@k All he'd done was make a bigger mess of the pan.
But I wanted to eat Dad's eggs, so I held my tongue and waited quietly at the table.
The plate he brought over held what could loosely be described as an egg-shaped object, thoroughly mangled.
There were traces of an adventurous attempt to mix in cheese, but everything had burned beyond redemption.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Still, the aroma was savory and appetizing.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: The toaster popped, delivering thick-cut toast.
Dad liked his drinks sweet, so I made café au lait with plenty of milk and sugar.
Laid out on the table, the spread looked like a disastrous home-ec project by elementary schoolers... but with Dad in his apron, doing his earnest best, it all shone beautifully.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``Mm.@k@vS09/02/170200387. It smells great.@k@vS09/02/170200388. Now let's see how it tastes, ahaha.``
``Maybe you should do the cooking after all...@k@vS09/00/172900043. I feel bad serving you this...``
``I'll take over cooking again once you're busy with work.``
Until now, talk of work had been taboo between us.
Dad hadn't held a job since the divorce.
It wasn't only the divorce.
The enormous settlement Mom had left behind meant he didn't have to.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: And my guilt over the divorce had made me tolerate his idleness.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: But... Dad had cleared his wrongdoing through last night's repentance.
...And the sin I'd carried for doing nothing during Mom's time... I had atoned for it myself, through yesterday's actions.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Now, neither of us carried any sin.
``I'm... going to look for a job.@k@vS09/00/172900046. I'm thinking of going to the employment office today.``
``Yeah.@k@vS09/02/170200391. That's a great idea.``
It wasn't about the money.@k Dad needed a normal routine.
``...At my age and without much stamina... I'm not sure I can find anything decent...``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``What kind of work would you like?``
``A desk job would be ideal, but they don't exactly hand those out to men my age.``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``I can ask my friend.@k@vS09/02/170200394. Mii-chan's family are major landowners around here, so she might be able to put in a word.``
``No, no, I'll find something myself.@k@vS09/00/172900050. I need to be that much of a grown-up, at least.``
``Ahahahaha.``
When was the last time we'd enjoyed such a lively breakfast conversation?
...For that matter, when had we last even eaten breakfast together?
I'd forgotten how refreshing the morning light through the window could be.
...I wished I could linger in this moment forever.@k But unfortunately, school awaited.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: I tried playing the spoiled daughter, saying I'd skip school if he took me somewhere fun. But Dad seized the chance to play the proper father and insisted I had to go.
``Well... I'm off, then.``
``Right. Have a good day.``
When I headed for the front door, he followed to see me off.@k It was a little embarrassing, somehow.
While I was tying my shoes, he spoke.
...He hadn't come to see me off—he'd come because there was something he needed to say.

``...I've been lonely ever since the divorce.@k@vS09/00/172900054. But I realized—I have you, Reina. I don't need to be lonely.@k@vS09/00/172900055. That's right. There was never any reason to feel lonely in the first place.``
``Dad... isn't that what they call being a family?``
``...You're right.@k@vS09/00/172900057. ...Having my own daughter teach me something like that—I'm really hopeless.``
``Well, Keiichi-kun is waiting for me, so I'd better go.``

``Have a good day.@k@vS09/00/172900059. Oh, Reina.@k@vS09/00/172900060. I'm cooking dinner tonight.@k@vS09/00/172900061. I'll pick up groceries on the way back from the employment office.@k@vS09/00/172900062. I promise it won't be like breakfast. Look forward to it.``
``Ahahahaha, okay!@k@vS09/02/170200399. I'm looking forward to it.@k@vS09/02/170200400. I'm off!``
Heading to school, seen off by Dad in his apron... this was the first step of our new life.
I had finally reclaimed it.
Everything that had gone wrong after we left Hinamizawa—I had finally taken it all back.
Mom wasn't here, true... but Dad and I were family, and there was nothing to feel lonely about.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: The cicadas' lively chorus sounded as if it were celebrating the start of my new life.
The sunlight was bright, the breeze was fresh.
The only thing not so fresh was... maybe the clock? I was running late.
I'd spent too long talking with Dad and was now quite behind schedule.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Keiichi-kun might not have waited and gone on ahead.
...But he was there.
``Hey!@k@vS09/01/170100257. Good morning!``
``Ahahaha, good morning~!@k@vS09/02/170200402. Sorry I'm late, sorry!``
``Don't worry about it.@k@vS09/01/170100259. I'm usually the one making you wait.@k@vS09/01/170100260. Let me be the one waiting for a change.@k@vS09/01/170100261. Wahahaha!``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``We're really late now.@k@vS09/02/170200404. We should probably walk fast, don't you think?``
``Yeah.@k@vS09/01/170100263. Let's pick up the pace.@k@vS09/01/170100264. Otherwise we'll end up running.``
``I bet Mii-chan already went ahead.``
``She's waiting. No question.@k@vS09/01/170100264_2. We're friends, right?@k@vS09/01/170100264_3. We'd never abandon each other or go on without someone.``
``By the way, did something good happen this morning?``
``Huh?@k@vS09/02/170200408. Why do you ask?``
``I dunno, you seem even more cheerful than usual.@k@vS09/01/170100267. Figured maybe something special happened.``
My good mood must have been so obvious that Keiichi-kun spotted it at a glance.
I had my family, and I had my friends.
I felt something warm spreading through my chest.
``...Ahahahaha.@k@vS09/02/170200410. Oh, listen to this, listen.@k@vS09/02/170200411. Dad tried to make breakfast this morning, and... ahahahaha...``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``What? That's how you make eggs, isn't it?@k@vS09/01/170100269. Salad oil is for salads, right...?``
``What?!@k@vS09/02/170200413. Ahahaha!@k@vS09/02/170200414. I am so telling Mii-chan and everyone!``
``You said what?!``
``Such a remark is utterly unforgivable!!``
``Bring it on! If you're that outraged, you must be ready for a showdown!``
``Ooh, things are heating up!@k@vS09/03/170300144. Hey, Satoko, Kei-chan—how about a side bet on today's game?``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``Mii-chan, what's a side bet?``

``Ah, a side bet means@k@vS09/01/170100272. Satoko and I would wager against each other on the side.@k@vS09/01/170100273. Whoever ranks lower in the club game takes an extra penalty on top of the normal one!``
``...In other words, no matter who wins or loses, I get to enjoy the show.``
``Heh heh heh!@k@vS09/03/170300146. What do you say, Satoko~?``
``Oh, how delightful!@k@vS09/04/170400041. Keiichi-san will lose the game and take the punishment, then lose to me and take a double punishment—how marvelous!``
``Alright, Satoko accepts the challenge!``
``Then it's decided—today's game comes with a side bet!``
``...Thinking up the punishment is more fun than choosing the game itself.``
``Sure is!@k@vS09/01/170100275. I can already picture the look on Satoko's face after her punishment!``
``I believe you're confusing yourself with me, Keiichi-san!!!``
``Ouch! Take that! How dare you! Hyah!``
``Hey, you two—the game hasn't even started yet!``
The classroom door rattled open as Chie-sensei appeared.
``Sorry for the wait, everyone.@k@vS09/22/171400003. Let's begin the closing homeroom.``
The classmates who'd been scattered about returned to their seats.
``Mii-chan.``
``Hm? What?``
``I hate to ruin the mood when Keiichi-kun and Satoko-chan are so fired up, but...@k@vS09/02/170200418. ...sorry. I've got things to do again today, so I need to head straight home...``
``Oh... really?@k@vS09/03/170300152. Well, if you've got things to do, can't be helped.@k@vS09/03/170300153. Housework?``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``Yeah.``
``Housework—well, this old man has no right to complain about that.@k@vS09/03/170300155. ...We have our maids handle everything at our place.``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``You should pitch in sometimes, Mii-chan.@k@vS09/02/170200421. It'd be good bridal training.``
``Rest assured.@k@vS09/03/170300157. All such skills have already been duly acquired.@k@|@y@vS09/03/170300158. Ahyahyahyahya.``
Mii-chan looks like someone who avoids chores, but she can actually do anything. That's what makes her impressive.
She worked hard to learn all of that,@k yet acts as though it came effortlessly. It's very Mii-chan.@k I find it endearing.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``What—seriously, Rena?@k@vS09/01/170100277. You're going home?``
``Oh, then you'll miss Keiichi-san going to the teachers' office in something embarrassing?``
``...It's going to be quite a spectacle. What a shame.``
``...Yeah, sorry.``
``You went straight home for housework yesterday too.``
``Did something happen?``
``Yeah, kind of. All sorts of things.@k@vS09/02/170200423. It might take a few more days.``
``Hmm.@k@vS09/01/170100281. So you won't be at club for a while, then.``
``...Probably not.@k@vS09/02/170200425. I'll do my best to come back as soon as I can.``
``Is it something we could help with?@k@vS09/01/170100283. Like, if you need to move heavy furniture or something, just say the word.@k@vS09/01/170100284. I'll come running!``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``Redecorating?@k@vS09/03/170300160. This old man can round up plenty of help.``
``Ohoho!@k@vS09/04/170400046. What if we turned helping Rena-san into a competition?``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``...The person who ends up not helping will probably win.``
``Ahahaha, thanks everyone.@k@vS09/02/170200427. I appreciate the thought.``

``Well, can't be helped then!@k@vS09/03/170300162. Rena Ryuugu is hereby declared withdrawn from the front lines as of today!@k@vS09/03/170300163. You are granted leave until your domestic duties are complete. I order you to rest, recover your fighting spirit, and return to the front!``
``Besides, we've got plenty of four-player games!@k@vS09/03/170300165. Oh hey, Kei-chan—can you play mahjong?``
``Leave it to me!@k@vS09/01/170100286. My old man sometimes drags me into games with his work buddies!``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``Ohohoho!@k@vS09/04/170400048. We are quite proficient at mahjong as well!``
After that, the club members carried on with their usual lively racket.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: I took one more look at my wonderful friends, engraved the certainty that my place among them would always be there, and left the classroom.
Once I got home, I'd bike out toward Yagouchi.
...I had to hurry with the remaining work.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Ideally, I wanted to finish everything before nightfall.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Then everything would go back to normal.
``I'm a little worried that the tiles have bamboo backs.@k@vS09/01/170100288. You haven't marked them, have you?``
``Who knows?@k@vS09/04/170400050. Keiichi-san, you sound traumatized by what happened during our card game.``
``Well, they're cheap tiles, so there might be some scratches.@k@vS09/03/170300167. But that's just natural wear.``
``A little uncertainty is what makes our club what it is, right?@k@vS09/03/170300169. Heh heh heh!``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``...Mii, how many red tiles are we using?@k@vS09/05/170500039. Six?``
...Why did Rika-chan's practiced ease look so terrifyingly formidable?
Oh well.@k Let me start by checking the tiles.
...Family mahjong is one thing, but this is club—you have to be vigilant from the start!
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Otherwise, you might miss something like a missing tile, with five of another kind hidden in the set.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``We wouldn't do that!``
``...But that's an interesting idea.``
``Hmm, so there's a trick like that...@k@vS09/03/170300173. Interesting, interesting...``
``Right, let's flip all the tiles face-up and line them up.@k@vS09/01/170100290. Come on, come on.``
Everyone turned the tiles face-up and sorted them like a number grid.
``Huh?@k@vS09/01/170100292. Hey, is someone hiding a red dragon over there?``
``We're missing one six-pin and one six-bamboo.``
``...Oh my, we're short a six-character too.``
``What's going on?@k@vS09/01/170100294. There are way too many missing tiles.``
``...Oh! That's right, I remember now. Hold on a second.``
Mion slapped her palm, stood up, moved her chair aside, and stuck a finger into a gap in the floorboards.
As we watched in bewilderment, she plucked out a single tile.
``Here's the red dragon.``
``Now where did I put the other three...``
``Hold on, Mion.@k@vS09/01/170100296. Why are there mahjong tiles hidden all over the classroom...?``
``Nyahahaha...@k@vS09/03/170300178. Well, you know... ahyahyahyahya...!``
Apparently, Mion had hidden three tiles somewhere in the classroom and forgotten where.
Three missing tiles made the game impossible.
I suggested switching to a different game, but Mion insisted that missing club equipment was unacceptable and proposed turning the tile search into a competition.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``As long as I can compete with Keiichi-san, I don't care what the game is, but...@k@vS09/04/170400054. ...I'd prefer something a bit more proper.``
``Same here.@k@vS09/01/170100298. Plus, we don't even know if they're actually in this classroom, right?``
``I'm pretty sure they are.``
``...Or maybe I put them in my pocket and forgot, and they're sitting by the washing machine at home... maybe?``
...Note to self: always count the tiles after playing mahjong with Mion.
At my muttered observation, everyone except Mion nodded emphatically.
``What~?@k@vS09/03/170300183. You don't want to play the classroom tile hunt?``
``That's just cleaning up after your cheating, Mion.@k@vS09/01/170100300. Do it yourself.@k@vS09/01/170100301. Don't co-opt club time for personal cleanup.``
``Fine, fine.@k@vS09/03/170300185. So what do we play?``
``Mahjong's out.@k@vS09/03/170300187. Something else?@k@vS09/03/170300188. Anyone have ideas?``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Mion almost always picked the game using her authority as club president.
Being put on the spot like this, none of us could think of anything.
While we racked our brains, Mion clapped her hands again.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``Hey, everyone.@k@vS09/03/170300190. If you don't want to hunt for tiles,@k@vS09/03/170300191. how about a full-scale treasure hunt?@k@vS09/03/170300192. And when you think treasure hunting, you think Rena!``
``You mean the junk heaps at the dam site?``
``I don't go there often, but it's quite big, isn't it?``
``...They say the ghost of the dismemberment murder victim haunts that place. We should stay away.``
``Ahahaha!@k@vS09/03/170300194. A ghost? That only makes it better!@k@vS09/03/170300195. A treasure mountain needs that kind of atmosphere!``
``There's a rumor like that?@k@vS09/01/170100304. ...Right, the main suspect is still at large.@k@vS09/01/170100305. And one of the victim's arms was never found, right?``
``...They say the construction manager's ghost wanders the site, searching for his missing arm.``
``Oh my, Rika!@k@vS09/04/170400057. Don't tell me you're afraid of ghosts!@k@vS09/04/170400058. At your age, that's embarrassing!``
``...I'm a shrine maiden. I can see them whether I want to or not...``
``Whoa... you mean like a sixth sense...?@k@vS09/01/170100307. Come to think of it, I've heard that people with the gift see ghosts as naturally as anything.``
``How ridiculous!@k@vS09/04/170400060. I've been living with Rika for ages and I've never seen anything like that!``
``...That's just because you can't see them, Satoko.@k@vS09/05/170500044. ...When you're asleep in your futon at night, I always see...``
``R-Rika!@k@vS09/04/170400062. Don't just smile and trail off like that!!@k@vS09/04/170400063. What do you see when I'm sleeping?!!!``
...Poor Satoko.
She was putting on a brave face now while it was still light out, but tonight, it'd creep up on her.
...Maybe I should make a silent phone call tonight, just to give her a scare. Heh heh heh!
``Myself included, I don't think anyone voluntarily visits that place since the dam conflict ended.``

``That's entirely plausible!``
``Hau~, I'm taking you hooome~!! she'd say.``
Wahahaha!
The image was so easy to picture that everyone burst out laughing.
